description: Distribute balls with overlaps until a target porosity is achieved. Ball positions and radii are drawn from uniform distributions (or Gaussian/FISH distributions). Significant ball overlaps will likely result from this operation. This command contrasts with ball generate where balls are generated without overlap.

bin_keywords.name: gauss
bin_keywords.description: Use Gaussian distribution with mean=(radlow+radhi)/2, std=(radhi-radlow)/2. Optional f (default 0.1) sets minimum radius

bin_keywords.name: fish-distribution
bin_keywords.description: Use FISH function to generate radii. Cannot be used with gauss or radius

bin_keywords.name: volume-fraction
bin_keywords.description: Volume fraction for this bin (all bins must sum to 1.0)

python_sdk_alternative.workaround: itasca.command('ball distribute ...') - no Python SDK equivalent for porosity-based distribution with overlaps

keywords.name: volume-fraction
keywords.description: Specify the distributional properties of bin i . Any number of distributions with radii ranges, volume fractions, and distributional types can be specified. The volume fractions of all distributions must sum to 1. The ball radii are drawn from the distribution represented by the FISH function s . Function arguments can be specified as though no function arguments are required. The FISH function must return a floating point value that is the ball radius. A FISH distribution cannot be specified with the gauss or radius keywords. Specify that the ball radii are chosen from a Gaussian distribution with mean value ( fradlow + fradhi )/2 and standard deviation ( fradhi - fradlow )/2. The optional f (default 0.1), multiplied by fradlow , constitutes the minimum radius allowed. Specify that generated balls are given group name s at slot slot . If the slot keyword is not specified, then the group name is assigned to the slot Default . Specify the radius range to be used during generation. If fradhi is not specified, then fradhi = fradlow . By default fradhi = fradlow = 1.0. Cannot be given with the fish-distribution keyword. The volume fraction of balls in this distribution. The sum of volume fractions of all distributions must be 1.

keywords.name: box
keywords.description: Ball will fully fall within this box, such that they will not overlap the box faces or edges. If the y,z -boundaries are omitted, they will be taken as equal to the \(x\) -boundaries. If the \(z\) -boundaries are omitted, they will be taken equal to the \(y\) -boundaries. By default, the box is the model domain.

keywords.name: number-bins
keywords.description: Number of distributions used to generate the assembly.

keywords.name: porosity
keywords.description: The final porosity is set to f . By default, f = {0.16 in 2D; 0.359 in 3D} corresponding to the porosity for a tight, mono-disperse packing of {disks in 2D; spheres in 3D}.

keywords.name: resolution
keywords.description: Optional resolution that can be used as a multiplicative factor of the radii of each ball generated. By default, f = 1.0.

examples.description: Distribute balls with radius 1.0-1.6 to achieve 30% porosity (allows overlaps)

examples.command: ball distribute number-bins 2 bin 1 radius 0.5 1.0 volume-fraction 0.6 bin 2 radius 1.0 1.5 volume-fraction 0.4 porosity 0.35
examples.description: Two size distributions: 60% small balls (0.5-1.0), 40% large balls (1.0-1.5)

examples.command: ball distribute radius 1.0 1.5 gauss 0.1 porosity 0.32 box -10 10 -10 10 -5 5
examples.description: Gaussian distribution in specified box region

examples.command: ball distribute number-bins 1 bin 1 radius 0.8 1.2 group 'layer1' volume-fraction 1.0 porosity 0.38
